title: quadlet podman-systemd.unit failing to recognize Network=$name.container format for .container
body:
### Issue Description

Hello, I'm trying to run Immich with Tailscale via rootless podman quadlets in Fedora CoreOS Stable stream.
I'm having a weird issue and want to see if I am missing anything as I've been looking over the documentation. For some reason systemd is not recognizing the `.container` file when I set `Network=tailscale.container`. I'm following the documentation which states

> Another special case is that if the name ends with .container, the container will reuse the network stack of another container created by $name.container. The generated systemd service contains a dependency on $name.service.


Podman version
```core@immich03:~/quadlets/user$ podman -v
podman version 5.3.1
```

.container files.
```
[Unit]
Description=Immich Server
Requires=immich-redis.service immich-postgres.service
After=immich-redis.service immich-postgres.service

[Container]
ContainerName=immich_server
Image=ghcr.io/imagegenius/immich:noml
AutoUpdate=registry
EnvironmentFile=/home/core/data/conf/immich_server.env
Network=tailscale.container
NetworkAlias=immich_server
Volume=immich_server-config:/config
Volume=/var/mnt/immich/photos:/photos
Volume=/var/mnt/immich/libraries:/libraries

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

```
[Unit]
Description=Tailscale

[Container]
ContainerName=Tailscale
Image=docker.io/tailscale/tailscale:latest
AutoUpdate=registry
HostName=immich
Network=immich
NetworkAlias=tailscale
Volume=tailscale_data:/var/lib/tailscale
EnvironmentFile=/home/core/data/conf/tailscale.env
AddDevice=/dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun
AddCapability=CAP_NET_ADMIN

[Service]
Restart=always
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run CoreOS Stable in a VM
2. Try to run two containers via quadlets with one container reusing the network of another via specifying the `Network=$name.container` format
3. systemd fails to recognize the unit file.


### Describe the results you received

systemd is not recognizing the .container unit file to control services

Example showing 'Loaded: not-found' after running `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
```
core@immich03:~/quadlets/user$ systemctl --user status immich-server.service
× immich-server.service
     Loaded: not-found (Reason: Unit immich-server.service not found.)
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Thu 2025-01-30 20:18:43 UTC; 22min ago
 Invocation: 7457bf4574184c92964b011cc8a4750f
   Main PID: 4681 (code=exited, status=125)
```

### Describe the results you expected

systemd recognizes the Network=tailscale.container options correctly and loads the unit

Example showing 'Loaded: loaded'

```
core@immich03:~/quadlets/user$ systemctl --user status immich-server.service
× immich-server.service - Immich Server
     Loaded: loaded (/var/home/core/.config/containers/systemd/immich-server.container; generated)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/user/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Thu 2025-01-30 20:18:43 UTC; 20min ago
 Invocation: 7457bf4574184c92964b011cc8a4750f
   Main PID: 4681 (code=exited, status=125)
```

comments.body:
I've just tried the same with `5.3.1` and it seems to work for me:
```
○ immich-server.service - Immich Server
     Loaded: loaded (/home/yblum.linux/.config/containers/systemd/25174/immich-server.container; generated)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/user/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: inactive (dead)
```

A few question:
1. I assume the name of the first `.container` file is `immich-server.container` because you expect the service unit to be `immich-server.service`. Is that true?
2. What is the name of the second `.container`? In order for it to work, it must be the same name reference to. That it `tailscale.container` (or update the reference)
3. Can you please run `/home/yblum.linux/work/podman/bin/quadlet --user --dryrun` and check that there are no errors and that `immich-server.service` was in fact created?
4. If you have many Quadlet files you can also put the two in their own directory and set the environment variable `QUADLET_UNIT_DIRS` when running the `dryrun`

Hello thanks for helping. Sorry I forgot to add that output but yes the container is called `tailscale.container`. 

```
core@immich03:~/quadlets/user$ ll
total 16
-rw-r--r--. 1 core core 727 Jan 30 03:39 immich-postgres.container
-rw-r--r--. 1 core core 305 Jan 30 03:35 immich-redis.container
-rw-r--r--. 1 core core 526 Jan 30 20:41 immich-server.container
-rw-r--r--. 1 core core 365 Jan 30 19:41 tailscale.container
```

core@immich03:~/quadlets/user$ systemctl --user status tailscale.service
● tailscale.service - Tailscale
     Loaded: loaded (/var/home/core/.config/containers/systemd/tailscale.container; generated)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/user/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf

   Main PID: 9706 (conmon)
      Tasks: 25 (limit: 4507)
     Memory: 25.4M (peak: 31.2M)
        CPU: 490ms

             ├─libpod-payload-45f0fe7ef8327afa83421a05e3720aebf2d79f05da1778b6ffac45eb9e29bc9e
             │ ├─9708 /usr/local/bin/containerboot
             │ └─9716 tailscaled --socket=/tmp/tailscaled.sock --statedir=/var/lib/tailscale --tun=userspace-ne>
             └─runtime
               └─9706 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 45f0fe7ef8327afa83421a05e3720aebf2d79f05da1778b6ffac45>

```
---tailscale.service---
[Unit]
Wants=podman-user-wait-network-online.service
After=podman-user-wait-network-online.service
Description=Tailscale
SourcePath=/var/home/core/.config/containers/systemd/tailscale.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[X-Container]
ContainerName=Tailscale
Image=docker.io/tailscale/tailscale:latest
AutoUpdate=registry
HostName=immich
Network=immich
NetworkAlias=tailscale
Volume=tailscale_data:/var/lib/tailscale
EnvironmentFile=/home/core/data/conf/tailscale.env
AddDevice=/dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun
AddCapability=CAP_NET_ADMIN

[Service]
Restart=always
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name Tailscale --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --hostname immich --network-alias tailscale --network immich --sdnotify=conmon -d --device /dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun --cap-add cap_net_admin -v tailscale_data:/var/lib/tailscale --label io.containers.autoupdate=registry --env-file /home/core/data/conf/tailscale.env docker.io/tailscale/tailscale:latest

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

quadlet-generator[10021]: converting "immich-server.container": cannot get the resource name of tailscale.container
```

Hi,

I accidentally tested with the wrong version (my build off of `main`) and this is why it worked for me. While this feature does exist in `v5.3.1`, this version has an issue that was resolved here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24794. 

The root cause of the issue was the processing order and  the fix will be part of `v5.4.X`.

In the meanwhile what you can do is rename `tailscale.container` to make sure that it is processed before `immich-server.container` (e.g. `01-tailscale.container`) and update the dependency accordingly.
I do have the same issue but prefixing the container with 01 does not fix it for me.

> I do have the same issue but prefixing the container with 01 does not fix it for me.

This workaround also depends on where the files are located. Are the two in the same directory? 

In any case, keep in mind that this is just a trial-and-error workaround for an issue that was already resolved and just waiting to get released
Hmm okay, do you have an ETA on when that release is coming out?

> Hmm okay, do you have an ETA on when that release is coming out?

I don't know for sure. But rc-2 was already tagged for 5.4.0. So, I assume in the next few weeks (but, again, not sure)
I tested the rc-2 and I can confirm that fixes my issue.
